channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: At rear 96 Lucey Way SE16, 12th July
item.description:
Gathered over 3 days

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lucey Way, Bermondsey, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Various piles of fly tip, 12th July
item.description:
Various piles of fly tip along church manorway near the Sewell road end. Piles of green waste and other rubbish. There’s at at least 2 or 3 piles also the whole area could do with a good litter pick and sweep.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Church Manorway, Abbey Wood
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Household stuff, 12th July
item.description:
This was on the doorstep of the closest house in The Green outside there front door for a few days, now it's by the tree with the school kids kicking it around.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): The Green, Falconwood, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped items, 12th July
item.description:
Next to St David’s mansions again.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 172, Dunstan's Road, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bus shelter, 12th July
item.description:
Wells park Road outside Sydenham Hill Rstate. Remove graffiti from bus shelter

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Wells Park Road, Forest Hill, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Graffiti / Flyposting on stop or shelter (non-offensive)

item.title: Residents Unable to Sleep Due to Loose Manhole Covers, 12th July
item.description:
The three manhole covers outside 681 Commercial Road, by Tequila Wharf They were not reinstated correctly after the recent works and now slam loudly every time traffic passes over them. The constant banging is keeping residents awake throughout the night, and with the current hot weather forcing windows to stay open, it has become impossible to get any sleep. This problem needs to be dealt with today. Please send a team to secure the covers properly.
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Urgent: Unbearable Night-Time Noise from Loose Manhole Covers, 12th July
item.description:
Please urgently attend to the three manhole covers outside 681 Commercial Road, near Tequila Wharf and John Nash Mews. Since recent works, the covers appear to have been left improperly seated. Every passing vehicle creates a loud metal banging noise, making it impossible for residents to sleep. With windows open due to the hot weather, the constant noise is unbearable and continues throughout the night. As this is a busy main road, please arrange for the covers to be secured immediately.
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 3 manhole covers extremely noisy not in place, 12th July
item.description:
​Dear Teams, ​Please resolve an urgent noise issue outside 681 Commercial Road, outside Tequila Wharf and John Nash Mews. ​Contractors recently left a manhole cover improperly seated. Every passing vehicle causes a loud, disruptive metal banging sound that is keeping all residents at the front of the building awake as we also have to leave windows open in the heatwave this is very disturbing. ​As this is a major thoroughfare, please send a team to secure the cover immediately. ​ This was reported last week Reported at 15:22, Thursday 9 July 2026 it is not Staurday and these are still not fixed

Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Broken breeze blocks. Goal pist, Metal poles, Sponge, Material, Metal items, Debris, 12th July
item.description:
An eyesore on the green again by weighing kids.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 25, Clarendon Green, St Paul's Cray, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Asylum road is always full of litter, 12th July
item.description:
The street and road is highly popular and doesn't get clean regularly enough. We feel ashamed of the conditions of the footpath which is full of litter everyday. More must be done more regularly and public bins installed accross the street to prevent the littering.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 103-147, Asylum Road, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Voi parking is a complete mess, no pavement for access, spillage into road, 12th July
item.description:
Only Voi allows parking on this corner clearly. The bikes are everywhere, blocking the entire path and into particular the disabled access and raised tiles. The bikes are mostly toppled and have spilled into the road. This has been an issue for weeks now, and has only grown, as VOI have not done anything about it.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Croft Street, New Cross Gate, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Full black rubbish bag beside bin west side pavement, 12th July
item.description:
Rubbish bag beside bin
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Elmers End Road, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Huge number of bikes cluttering street, 12th July
item.description:
Insane number of voi bikes cluttering the pavement and road!! There are way too many and extremely disorderly.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Croft Street, New Cross Gate, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Broken glass on both sides of pavement and in street. Bottles lying around on grass verge, 12th July
item.description:
Broken glass. We can’t safely walk our dog in any direction
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Peckarmans Wood, Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Recurring dumping, 12th July
item.description:
Outside Kingshold community centre on the public pavement there is recurring dumping of clothes almost every day for months now, which is a hazard for pedestrians and attracts other dumpers

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Ainsworth Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Over grown bush onto the pavement, 12th July
item.description:
The bush has caused obstruction of the path way for pedestrians to walk its covering the pavement

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Tuskar Street, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumping of dog litter on the side public wall, 12th July
item.description:
Dog walkers are dumping dog waste in bags on the side wall and this has been happening for months now
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Tuskar Street, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: A bin full of flys and maggots, 12th July
item.description:
A bin crawling with maggots with an awful smell when you walk past.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1, Oaks Avenue, West Norwood, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
